title: Tapping to Release Remaining Doubts About Wealth
subtitle: A tapping practice for clearing the quiet residual doubts that persist after financial progress
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 64 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the small, persistent doubts that stay even when things are going well — the 'what if it stops?' worry, the old fear that abundance might not last. The reframe moves toward settled trust in lasting prosperity.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 64 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when financial things are generally going well but a quiet background worry persists — the sense that it might not last, or that the progress is somehow fragile.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 64 of a 12-week money program. Week 10 theme: building unshakable money trust. The script targets the quiet residual doubt that sits underneath financial progress without announcing itself — the whisper that asks 'what if it slows down?' or 'what if I can't hold this?' The reframe moves toward deep trust in wealth as natural and lasting.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though a part of me still doubts big wealth is possible, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though small worries still whisper that money could stop, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though old fears sometimes show up, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I hear quiet doubts about lasting wealth.
Side of eye: What if things slow down?
Under eye: What if I can't keep it up?
Under nose: Old habits of worry resurface.
Chin: I'm ready to clear those last bits.
Collarbone: I've come so far already.
Under arm: I let go of every remaining doubt.
Top of head: I choose deep trust instead.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I know wealth is natural and constant.
Side of eye: I trust my ability to create and keep it.
Under eye: I believe money flows to me endlessly.
Under nose: I stand confident in lasting abundance.
Chin: I feel secure in every financial season.
Collarbone: I'm grateful for permanent prosperity.
Under arm: I let calm trust guide me every day.
Top of head: I live free of doubt about wealth.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 64 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
